name: Ruta de los Torreones – circular desde Aldealpozo
region_intro: Hiking trails around Campo De Gomara, located in the province of Soria, Spain, traverse a landscape primarily characterized by expansive, flat plains dedicated to cereal cultivation. The region also features the Moncayo mountain range, home to Pico Moncayo, which offers significant elevation and oak forests. Historical sites and smaller hills are interspersed throughout the area, providing varied points of interest. Best hiking trails around Campo De Gomara The most popular hiking route is Pico Moncayo desde Cuevas de Ágreda, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route ascends to the Moncayo Summit, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Capadocia Soriana – circular desde Aguaviva de la Vega, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This trail explores unique geological formations near Aguaviva de la Vega. Local hikers also love the Peña Negrilla (2.117 m) – circular desde Aldehuela de Ágreda por la Sierra del Moncayo, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra del Moncayo, often completed in about 5 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Campo De Gomara is defined by expansive plains, the Moncayo mountain range, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Campo De Gomara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Campo De Gomara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Campo De Gomara?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes in Campo De Gomara, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 9 easy trails, 21 moderate options, and 6 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect when hiking in Campo De Gomara?
A: Campo De Gomara is characterized by expansive, flat plains primarily used for cereal cultivation, offering a sense of openness and tranquility. However, the region also features the impressive Moncayo mountain range, with its significant elevations and beautiful oak forests, providing a contrasting experience to the plains.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy hikes. For a relaxed walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059007413/alto-navarro-circular-desde-cihuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Navarro – circular desde Cihuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.5 km long. These flatter routes are ideal for those seeking a gentle outdoor experience.

Q: Where can I find challenging hikes in the region?
A: For more challenging ascents, head towards the Moncayo mountain range. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057568211/pico-moncayo-desde-cuevas-de-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Moncayo desde Cuevas de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, a 11.9 km trail, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056705826/pena-negrilla-2-117-m-circular-desde-aldehuela-de-agreda-por-la-sierra-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negrilla (2.117 m) – circular desde Aldehuela de Ágreda por la Sierra del Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.2 km route, offer significant elevation gain and panoramic views from the summits.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Campo De Gomara?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, explore the unique geological formations on the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062476263/la-capadocia-soriana-circular-desde-aguaviva-de-la-vega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Capadocia Soriana – circular desde Aguaviva de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy a forest walk on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056716529/hayedo-de-la-hoyada-circular-desde-aldehuela-de-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hayedo de La Hoyada – circular desde Aldehuela de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Campo De Gomara?
A: Yes, the region offers trails that lead to beautiful water features. Consider the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057585100/cascada-del-pozo-de-las-truchas-y-pena-del-tio-fresquito-circular-desde-agreda-pr-so-20-y-20-1-senda-barranco-del-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to a charming waterfall.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest to see while hiking?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical and cultural sites. The region features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3743913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Vozmediano&lt;/a&gt;. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; for breathtaking views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Campo De Gomara?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from the peaceful plains to the challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to combine nature with historical exploration.

Q: Is Campo De Gomara suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: Most trails in Campo De Gomara are suitable for dogs, especially those in the open plains and less crowded mountain paths. Always ensure your dog is under control and check local regulations for specific areas, particularly around nature reserves or agricultural lands.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Campo De Gomara?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Campo De Gomara. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring or rich autumnal colors in the forests of Moncayo. Summers can be hot, especially on the open plains, while winters can bring cold temperatures and snow to higher elevations.

Q: Are there any forest hikes in Campo De Gomara?
A: Yes, particularly within the Moncayo mountain range. The route to Pico Moncayo passes through a beautiful oak forest, and trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059070187/hayedo-del-moncayo-la-marcuela-aldehuela-de-agreda-circular-desde-vozmediano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hayedo del Moncayo, La Marcuela, Aldehuela de Ágreda – circular desde Vozmediano&lt;/a&gt; offer a chance to experience the region's woodlands.

Q: Can I combine hiking with cultural visits in the area?
A: Absolutely. Campo De Gomara is dotted with historical sites that can be integrated into your hiking plans. Beyond the castles, the region includes attractions like the Monasterio de Santa María de Huerta and the Church, Medieval Bridge, and Stele of Renieblas, which also boasts remains of a Roman camp.
tips: La Pica is an unpopulated area located in the municipality of Tajahuerce, in the province of Soria. There we will find a Romanesque church in a ruined state, the remains of a palace and a medieval tower, declared an asset of cultural interest. In the place we will find an information panel that will explain more details of the history that is hidden behind the desert.

This Roman bridge is part of road 257 that linked Astúrica and Caesaraugusta. It is a beautiful structure with two arches built of ashlar and masonry. The bridge was declared an asset of cultural interest in the province of Soria.
Currently uninhabited, Masegoso has a Berber tower from the 10th century and a beautiful Roman fountain.
